explanations.1: Regional anatomy focuses on the interrelationships of all structures in a specific body part, such as the ankle and foot. Systemic anatomy, by contrast, would focus on a single system like the skeletal system throughout the entire body.
explanations.2: Physiology focuses on the chemistry and physics of how structures work together to support life functions. A scientist studying metabolic and fluid changes during physical exertion is practicing exercise physiology.
explanations.3: The cell is considered the smallest independently functioning unit of a living organism. While atoms and molecules are smaller, they are not considered independently functioning biological units.
explanations.4: An organ is an anatomically distinct structure of the body composed of two or more tissue types that work together to perform a specific function. Tissues are the level of organization below organs, and organ systems are the level above.
explanations.5: The skeletal system provides the primary structural framework for the body and works with the muscular system to enable movement. While the muscular system is also involved in movement, the skeletal system is the foundational support structure.
explanations.6: Metabolism is defined as the sum of all anabolic and catabolic chemical reactions that take place in the body. Differentiation refers to the specialization of cells, while reproduction refers to the formation of new cells or organisms.
explanations.7: ATP is the primary energy-currency molecule of the cell because it stores energy in high-energy phosphate bonds for use in cellular processes. While it is produced during catabolism, its vital role is the controlled storage and release of that energy.
explanations.8: Differentiation is the process by which unspecialized cells become specialized in structure and function. Because cancer cells remain 'generic' and do not take on specific roles, they lack this characteristic of functional human life.
explanations.9: Brain cells require a constant supply of oxygen to produce ATP; brain damage occurs within minutes of deprivation. Survival without water is measured in days, and survival without food is measured in weeks.
explanations.10: All classes of nutrients—including water, macronutrients, and micronutrients—are essential for human survival and function. Carbohydrates and proteins are macronutrients, not micronutrients, and some micronutrients (like fat-soluble vitamins) can be stored by the body.
explanations.11: To maintain homeostasis in the cold, the body increases metabolic rate by breaking down stored energy to generate heat. The body would decrease, not increase, blood flow to the hands and feet to prevent heat loss from the core.
explanations.12: In a feedback loop, sensors detect a stimulus and send a signal to a control center, which then compares the value to the set point. The control center then signals an effector to cause a change.
explanations.13: The heat-loss center in the brain triggers effectors like sweat glands to increase output and skin blood vessels to dilate. Constricting blood vessels and slow breathing would actually help conserve heat rather than lose it.
explanations.14: Childbirth is a classic example of a positive feedback loop where the results of a change (oxytocin release and uterine contractions) further stimulate that change. Blood pressure and temperature regulation are maintained via negative feedback loops to ensure stability.
explanations.15: The standard anatomical position requires the person to be standing upright with feet shoulder-width apart, toes forward, and palms facing forward. None of the provided options (prone, supine, or arms at 90 degrees) accurately describe this position.
explanations.16: The sagittal plane divides the body or an organ vertically into right and left sides; if it runs directly down the middle, it is the midsagittal plane. A transverse plane would cut the banana into top and bottom pieces, and a coronal plane into front and back.
explanations.17: The lumbar region refers to the lower back, which is superior (higher) than the popliteal region (the back of the knee). It is superior to the gluteal region and inferior to the umbilical region.
explanations.18: The heart is located in the thoracic cavity, specifically within the mediastinum (the central compartment of the chest). The cranial cavity holds the brain, and the posterior cavity contains the brain and spinal cord.
explanations.19: Wilhelm Röntgen discovered X-rays in 1895, a breakthrough that allowed the first look inside the living body without surgery. This discovery earned him the first Nobel Prize in Physics.
explanations.20: Positron emission tomography (PET) scans use radiopharmaceuticals to illustrate physiologic activity, such as nutrient metabolism and blood flow. CT and MRI scans provide more static structural images rather than metabolic rates.
explanations.21: Ultrasonography is the least invasive imaging technique and uses no ionizing radiation, making it the safest choice for monitoring fetal growth. CT, PET, and X-rays all involve varying levels of radiation exposure.
explanations.22: MRI scans are very expensive and require significant shielding from the powerful magnetic signals they produce. Unlike X-rays or CT scans, MRIs do not use ionizing radiation, so they do not release radiation.
